mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS momentum are negative or insignificantly different from zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises the cost of capital and suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in retail sales and industrial production indicates a synchronized demand-supply slowdown that amplifies downside risk to corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS and INDPRO fails to predict negative 20-day returns with Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI inflation during a monetary tightening cycle leads to negative real earnings surprises and equity multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day SPX returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1200 thousand units signal durable goods demand and construction employment strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when HOUST > 1200.

mechanism_narrative: Falling real GDP growth combined with rising consumer price inflation compresses corporate profit margins and triggers valuation multiple contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL fails to produce negative 60-day returns with Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5 percent signals labor market deterioration that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE > 5.0.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales is a coincident indicator of consumer spending momentum that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive RSAFS growth are not significantly positive over a 2-year rolling window.
